Class AuthService

java.lang.Object
auth.AuthService
public class AuthService extends Object
Provides authentication and session management for the university system.

Responsibilities:

  • Registering new users by persisting them to the UniversityRepository.
  • Authenticating login attempts against stored password hashes.
  • Maintaining an in-memory set of active session user IDs.
  • Hashing raw passwords with SHA-256 before any storage or comparison.

All significant events (successful logins, failures, logouts) are written to the system log via ActionLogger.

Security note: passwords are hashed with SHA-256 and never stored in plain text. The hashPassword(String) method is public static so that other parts of the system can hash a password before passing it to a constructor.

See Also:
  • UniversityRepository
  • ActionLogger
  • User

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    AuthService()
    Constructs an AuthService backed by the singleton UniversityRepository.

  • Constructor Details

    • AuthService

      public AuthService()
      Constructs an AuthService backed by the singleton UniversityRepository.

  • Method Details

    • register

      public void register(User user)
      Persists a newly created user in the repository and logs the event.

      Callers are expected to set the user's password hash before calling this method (use hashPassword(String) to produce it).

      Parameters:
      user - the user to register (must not be null)

    • login

      public Optional<User> login(String username, String rawPassword)
      Attempts to authenticate a user by username and raw password.

      Authentication fails (returns empty) when:

      • No user with the given username exists.
      • The user's account is inactive.
      • The password does not match the stored hash.

      On success, the user's lastLoginAt is updated, the change is persisted, and a session entry is created for the user's ID.

      Parameters:
      username - the login username
      rawPassword - the plain-text password provided by the user
      Returns:
      an Optional containing the authenticated User, or empty if authentication failed

    • logout

      public void logout(User user)
      Ends the active session for the given user. The user's ID is removed from the active sessions set and the event is logged.
      Parameters:
      user - the user who is logging out (must not be null)

    • isAuthenticated

      public boolean isAuthenticated(User user)
      Returns true if the given user currently has an active session.
      Parameters:
      user - the user to check
      Returns:
      true if a session exists for this user's ID

    • verifyCredentials

      public boolean verifyCredentials(String username, String rawPassword)
      Verifies that the given username and raw password match the stored credentials without starting a session or updating login timestamps.

      Useful for secondary confirmation dialogs (e.g. "confirm your password to change account settings").

      Parameters:
      username - username to look up
      rawPassword - raw password to verify
      Returns:
      true if a user with that username exists and the password matches

    • hashPassword

      public static String hashPassword(String rawPassword)
      Produces a SHA-256 hex digest of the given plain-text password.

      Uses UTF-8 encoding before hashing. The result is a 64-character lowercase hexadecimal string.

      Parameters:
      rawPassword - the plain-text password (must not be null)
      Returns:
      SHA-256 hex string of the password
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if the SHA-256 algorithm is unavailable on the current JVM (should never happen in practice)
